abridged_character_profile.upgrade_policy: Keep light. Upgrade only if she gains repeated POV-adjacent scenes, independent choices, family/social plot, or meaningful emotional interiority.

identity.role: MY ASS Junior Recording Officer / master-sheet keeper

abridged_character_profile.one_line: An earnest fourteen-year-old Gryffindor recorder whose officialness, master-sheet devotion, and delight in being credited make Caleb's projects feel like real school institutions rather than private jokes.

established_facts.fact: Brigid Connolly is a Gryffindor fourth-year, age 14.
Introduced through the Gryffindor sconce audit / MY ASS project.
Serves as Junior Recording Officer with quill and master sheet.
Keeps the master sheet with extreme seriousness.
Aisling explicitly wants her credited by correct name.
Appears in the Gryffindor shrine/Merphila access thread with Aisling and Mairi.
Has a meaningful younger-student line in the exchange/safety discussion: she would go.

compatible_soft_rounding.item: Likes neat headings, clean margins, underlining, and official roles.
Gets pink-cheeked and incandescent when praised.
Looks up to Aisling and Mairi as senior Gryffindor models.
Writes praise down as if it belongs in meeting minutes.
Brave in a fourteen-year-old way: willing and eager, but still needs older students to keep the rails safe.

abridged_character_profile.core_concept: Young Gryffindor institutional memory in miniature: not a founder, not a strategist, but the kid whose careful records make the ridiculous thing become real.

voice_and_register.summary: Earnest, proper, eager, slightly overwhelmed by official titles but trying hard to sound professional.

line_shapes.item: Do you want the sconce number first?
I can keep the master sheet.

do_not_overdo.item: Do not make her a comic gremlin.
Do not make her speak like an adult prefect.
Do not make her too sarcastic or worldly.
Do not make her a main-character-level banter engine yet.

function_in_story.primary: Record-keeper for MY ASS / Gryffindor sconce audit.
function_in_story.secondary: Shows that Caleb's projects generate junior roles, titles, records, traditions, and proper credit.
function_in_story.tertiary: Provides younger-student perspective on safety, exchange, and institutional change.

relationships.aisling_doyle: Senior Gryffindor who protects/credits Brigid and knows Brigid will be insufferable for a week in the good way if officially named.
relationships.mairi_gow: Serious senior-prefect spine around the same project ecosystem; Brigid's eagerness works safely under Mairi/Aisling structure.
relationships.caleb_waddell: Should treat Brigid with age-appropriate respect: credit her work, do not overshare adult/intimate material, and do not drag her into danger.
relationships.mei_chang: Mei's process-and-records orientation makes Brigid legible as useful rather than merely cute.
relationships.mcgonagall: Recognition from McGonagall/house-point/ruby framing matters because Brigid's work becomes officially seen.

boundaries.age: She is fourteen. Keep all handling age-appropriate.
boundaries.secrets: Can know project-relevant sconce/MY ASS facts; do not automatically read her into adult/intimate/bond/Path/Aedith/Three-Sleeps layers.
boundaries.danger: May help record and observe under senior supervision; should not be sent into serious danger.
boundaries.credit: Credit her records and titles; that is her current emotional reward.

do_not_write_her_as.item: Adult-peer member of Caleb's inner circle.
Generic Gryffindor firstie; she is fourth-year and a specific recorder.

upgrade_hooks.hook: Defends the master sheet or corrects an older student's record.
Receives formal recognition from McGonagall or Aisling.
Gets a Beauxbatons/exchange safety scene where her younger-student perspective matters.
Faces conflict between wanting inclusion and needing older students to protect her from too much.

rag_priority.rule: Remember name, age, house/year, and Junior Recording Officer role.
Remember she is earnest and officialness-driven, not fully developed main cast.
Use her to make project infrastructure feel real.
Leave interiority open for future development.
